0.name: 2382 PVH-SO-PVa Otp Glut_5
0.rationale: PVH (paraventricular hypothalamus = PVN in MBA nomenclature, MBA:38) is the correct location. Glutamatergic and expresses Otp (orthopedia homeobox), a cardinal transcription factor for hypothalamic neuroendocrine neurons including PVN populations. Male_female_ratio = 2.7 (strongly male-biased), which aligns with the male-biased dimorphism of pvn_crfr1_neuron. Parent SUPT_0589. Top rank-0 candidate on combined location + sex ratio grounds.

0.name: 2331 LHA-AHN-PVH Otp Trh Glut_2

Trh co-expression overlaps with PVH neuroendocrine identity. Mixed region label (also includes LHA/AHN) reduces confidence — some cells in this cluster may be LHA or AHN rather than PVH. Plausible.

0.name: 1912 PVpo-VMPO-MPN Hmx2 Gaba_5

Male_female_ratio = 1.22 (slight male bias, weaker than expected dimorphism). Hmx2 TF identity is inconsistent with PVN Otp/Sim1 lineage. WEAK — wrong sub-region and wrong TF/NT profile.

GABAergic and Six6/Satb2+ TF profile inconsistent with PVN Otp/Sim1 neuroendocrine neurons. WEAK on location and NT type despite striking sex ratio.

1.name: 0585 PVH-SO-PVa Otp Glut_1
1.rationale: Highest DB score at rank-1 (score 3). PVH (paraventricular hypothalamus = PVN) label in region descriptor; Otp (orthopedia) is a cardinal TF for the Otp/Sim1 lineage that defines PVN neuroendocrine neurons; glutamatergic (correct NT type for PVH principal neurons); parent subcategory SUBC_133 contains multiple PVH-SO-PVa supertypes. This is the best single rank-1 candidate: correct region, correct TF lineage, correct NT type. No male_female_ratio available at supertype level but the neuroendocrine PVH Otp Glut lineage is the biologically appropriate match for CRFR1+ PVN neurons.

Parent of CLUS_2331 (ratio 2.45, male-biased). SUBC_131 (different subclass from SUBC_133). Plausible but mixed-region label reduces confidence — some cells may not be from PVH proper.

non_candidates_summary: The rank-0 DB query retrieved very few PVN (PVH) clusters — only CLUS_2331 and CLUS_2382 carry explicit PVH labels among the top candidates. This is a coverage limitation: the scoring function did not fully surface PVN-specific clusters likely because Crhr1, Esr1, and Ar are expressed across many hypothalamic regions. The two PVH-labeled rank-0 hits are the best candidates and both show appropriate male-biased sex ratios (2.45 and 2.7). The SBPV-PVa clusters (SUBT_0453-0455 and their children) show dramatically male-biased ratios (up to 32x in CLUS_1685) and are anatomically adjacent to PVN but are GABAergic with Six6/Satb2 TF identity — inconsistent with the Otp/Sim1 neuroendocrine PVN lineage expected for CRFR1+ neurons. At rank-1, SUPT_0585 (PVH-SO-PVa Otp Glut_1) is the clear top candidate: it is the only rank-1 entry with score 3, PVH label, and canonical Otp lineage. The broader SUBC_133 (PVH-SO-PVa Otp Glut supertypes) collectively spans the neuroendocrine PVH glutamatergic space and likely encompasses CRFR1-expressing neurons across multiple subtypes. BST-MPN, ZI, NTS, SPVI, STR, MEA, and thalamic clusters are all definitively excluded. NOTE: The immunohistochemistry-based PVN CRFR1 classification (Rosinger 2019) may encompass both GABAergic and glutamatergic PVN neurons — if CRFR1 is expressed on both compartments, some of the SBPV-adjacent GABAergic supertypes could be relevant as secondary candidates. Recommend curator confirm NT type from primary literature before excluding all GABAergic candidates.
